summary: BriefingForgeRoom is a MuranoOS "flagship epistemic room" that transforms dense source material (reports, cables, transcripts, open-source feeds) into audience-specific decision-grade briefing products without losing provenance, uncertainty, dissent, framing logic, or delivery discipline.
final_use_case: It turns briefing from a one-off document into a provenance-preserving production system: the same source base can support BLUFs, decision memos, live briefs, and decks without losing uncertainty or dissent.

instruments.input: entities, records, IDs, tables, JSON structures, indexes, and state transitions
instruments.operation: Discovery, creativity, systems thinking, and project generation. Use source library creativity/innovation/network sources to professionalize the project-development account. Vision: Explain Nexus and Personal OS as project-development and discovery infrastructure, not miscellaneous personal tooling. Mechanism: Extract creativity, network, learning, imagination, leadership, and project-generation mechanisms. Plan: Rewrite Nexus and planning pages around upstream/downstream project movement. Status: source library.

instruments.input: frontend, backend, storage, retrieval, auth, export, and portfolio linkage
instruments.operation: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. When to reach for this: You are adding a production-quality briefing / analyst-brief / decision-support room to a MuranoOS-style Tauri desktop app, need a real SQL schema and Zustand store architecture, and want the epistemic-foundation pattern where briefing products are generated projections rather than first-class documents.
